_comment: Note on userPromptSubmitted: registered since v0.25.0. Copilot CLI >= v1.0.65 and VS Code agent mode (Preview agent-hooks) inject the hook's JSON additionalContext into the model-facing prompt, so route-prompt's routing/plan-gate/security salience now reaches Copilot where hooks are enabled; sessionStart additionalContext is consumed the same way. Older Copilot versions ignore the JSON output -- harmless no-op (the docs.github.com hooks-reference 'Output processed: No' note lags the CLI changelog and VS Code docs; verify on your install with a canary prompt). route-prompt/session-start self-detect the surface: Claude Code events carry hook_event_name and get plain stdout; everything else gets the JSON shape. preToolUse is consumed as JSON permissionDecision deny, which is how guard.* enforces hard-blocks on Copilot. See docs/enforcement-surfaces.md for the guaranteed-vs-instructed matrix. The per-tool 'matcher' field is intentionally omitted (Copilot supports it, but): VS Code agent mode emits write tool names that cannot be fully enumerated (camelCase str_replace/insert/etc.), so guard.* must see every tool call and self-filter on file-path+content -- a 'matcher' regex like 'edit|create' would blind it to those VS Code writes and open a security gap. The fast self-exit on non-write calls is the deliberate cost of that coverage; do not add a matcher here.
